forward_patch:
--- original
+++ modified
@@ -0,0 +1,40 @@
+---
+assignees:
+- claude-code
+position_column: todo
+position_ordinal: '8880'
+title: 'Fix in-process MCP-server test timeouts: build the RMCP test client over a shared no-proxy reqwest client'
+---
+## What
+
+**Root cause (profiled 2026-05-21)** of the cluster of in-process-MCP-server test timeouts under full-workspace `cargo nextest run`: the cost is NOT in the server (a full server builds in ~20ms) — it is the **test-side RMCP client HTTP handshake**. `create_test_client` (`crates/swissarmyhammer-tools/src/mcp/test_utils.rs:35`) builds its `StreamableHttpClientTransport` over a fresh `reqwest::Client::default()`. On macOS, the first request of a fresh default `reqwest` client triggers `SystemConfiguration` system-proxy resolution via the single-threaded `configd` daemon — even for a literal `127.0.0.1` URL. Measured: default client first GET to loopback = **6.4s**; `reqwest::Client::builder().no_proxy()` first GET = **3.2ms**; warm default client = **0.4ms**.
+
+Because the proxy lookup funnels through one OS daemon, it **serializes across processes**. Under nextest's ~32-way parallelism, ~10+ in-process-server tests issue first-contact handshakes simultaneously and queue on `configd`, so per-startup wall time degrades from ~2s to 33–266s and crosses the 300s hard-kill. Confirmed mechanism: an 8-process × 4-concurrent harness reproduced 33–38s/startup; a multi_thread vs current-thread tokio runtime made no difference (bottleneck is below tokio, at the OS); and the degradation crosses process boundaries (rules out any in-process lock/CPU/spawn_blocking cause).
+
+## Fix
+
+Build the RMCP test client's transport over a `reqwest::Client` with proxy detection disabled, and share one client process-wide:
+
+- In `create_test_client` (`crates/swissarmyhammer-tools/src/mcp/test_utils.rs`): replace `reqwest::Client::default()` with a `reqwest::Client::builder().no_proxy().build()`, shared via a `OnceLock`/`once_cell` static so the proxy/connection setup is paid once per process, not per test. No proxy is ever meaningful for the loopback MCP server.
+- Verify whether this single change also eliminates the residual multi-second `serve()` handshake cost the profiler saw: that experiment warmed a SEPARATE client, but the transport uses its own `reqwest::Client::default()`, so the residual is likely the SAME proxy tax and should vanish once the transport client is `no_proxy`. MEASURE the handshake before/after.
+- ONLY if a real residual SSE-handshake cost remains after the no_proxy transport client: consider tuning the rmcp streamable-http config (`sse_retry`/`sse_keep_alive`) in `create_mcp_router` (`crates/swissarmyhammer-tools/src/mcp/unified_server.rs:718`). This touches PRODUCTION server behavior for all clients — do it cautiously, minimally, and only if measurement proves it necessary. Prefer leaving production behavior unchanged.
+
+### Also in this change (coherent cleanup)
+- **Revert the superseded builtin-library cache** (archived task `01KS5N6RZWN8BQBSYFVNF25G3P`): restore `crates/swissarmyhammer-prompts/src/prompt_resolver.rs`, `crates/swissarmyhammer-skills/src/skill_resolver.rs`, `crates/swissarmyhammer-agents/src/agent_resolver.rs`, `crates/swissarmyhammer-prompts/Cargo.toml`, and `Cargo.lock` to HEAD. The cache saved ~3.4ms of a 20ms server build (noise) and adds unjustified complexity. Confirm the workspace still builds after revert.
+- **Keep the `working_dir` isolation** already applied to `apps/swissarmyhammer-cli/tests/integration/mcp_integration.rs` (passing a tempdir so the server doesn't bind to/walk the host monorepo) — a genuine, independent improvement matching the existing `test_utils.rs` pattern.
+- **Keep removed**: the per-test `#[serial_test::serial(mcp_server)]` guards and the `.config/nextest.toml` `mcp_server` slow-timeout override. The whole point is that the real fix makes them unnecessary.
+
+## Acceptance Criteria
+- [ ] `create_test_client` builds its transport over a shared, proxy-disabled `reqwest::Client`; no per-call `reqwest::Client::default()` remains in that path.
+- [ ] Measured: a single in-process MCP server test's client handshake drops from seconds to tens of ms (record before/after).
+- [ ] `cargo nextest run --workspace` completes with the in-process MCP-server-startup cluster (`test_mcp_server_basic_functionality`/`_prompt_loading`/`_builtin_prompts`, `test_client_list_tools`/`test_client_call_tool`, kanban-app `state::tests` open-board tests) passing under load, with NO `serial_test` guards on those tests and NO `mcp_server` override in `.config/nextest.toml`. Run it 3 consecutive times (early-bail if any cluster test times out) to confirm reliability, not variance.
+- [ ] The superseded builtin cache is reverted; `cargo build --workspace` and `cargo clippy --workspace --all-targets -- -D warnings` are clean.
+- [ ] No production server behavior change unless measurement proves the rmcp SSE-config tuning is required; if made, it is minimal and documented.
+
+## Tests
+- [ ] A focused test (or reuse an existing cluster test) asserting/measuring that the client handshake to a loopback server completes quickly (e.g. well under 1s) — guards against a regression that reintroduces proxy detection.
+- [ ] Re-run in isolation: `cargo nextest run -p swissarmyhammer-cli -E 'test(test_mcp_server_basic_functionality) | test(test_mcp_server_prompt_loading) | test(test_mcp_server_builtin_prompts)'` and `cargo nextest run -p swissarmyhammer-tools -E 'test(test_client_list_tools) | test(test_client_call_tool)'` — all fast.
+- [ ] Full-workspace: 3 consecutive `cargo nextest run --workspace` with the cluster passing each time and the workarounds absent. Record honest timings.
+
+## Workflow
+- Use `/tdd` where practical — add the handshake-speed guard test first. Measure before/after for the handshake. Verify reliability with 3 consecutive full-workspace runs.
\ No newline at end of file

forward_patch:
--- original
+++ modified
@@ -9,32 +9,51 @@

 **Root cause (profiled 2026-05-21)** of the cluster of in-process-MCP-server test timeouts under full-workspace `cargo nextest run`: the cost is NOT in the server (a full server builds in ~20ms) — it is the **test-side RMCP client HTTP handshake**. `create_test_client` (`crates/swissarmyhammer-tools/src/mcp/test_utils.rs:35`) builds its `StreamableHttpClientTransport` over a fresh `reqwest::Client::default()`. On macOS, the first request of a fresh default `reqwest` client triggers `SystemConfiguration` system-proxy resolution via the single-threaded `configd` daemon — even for a literal `127.0.0.1` URL. Measured: default client first GET to loopback = **6.4s**; `reqwest::Client::builder().no_proxy()` first GET = **3.2ms**; warm default client = **0.4ms**.

-Because the proxy lookup funnels through one OS daemon, it **serializes across processes**. Under nextest's ~32-way parallelism, ~10+ in-process-server tests issue first-contact handshakes simultaneously and queue on `configd`, so per-startup wall time degrades from ~2s to 33–266s and crosses the 300s hard-kill. Confirmed mechanism: an 8-process × 4-concurrent harness reproduced 33–38s/startup; a multi_thread vs current-thread tokio runtime made no difference (bottleneck is below tokio, at the OS); and the degradation crosses process boundaries (rules out any in-process lock/CPU/spawn_blocking cause).
+Because the proxy lookup funnels through one OS daemon, it **serializes across processes**. Under nextest's ~32-way parallelism, ~10+ in-process-server tests issue first-contact handshakes simultaneously and queue on `configd`, so per-startup wall time degrades from ~2s to 33–266s and crosses the 300s hard-kill.

-## Fix
+## Fix (implemented)

-Build the RMCP test client's transport over a `reqwest::Client` with proxy detection disabled, and share one client process-wide:
+- `create_test_client` now builds its transport over a process-wide shared `reqwest::Client::builder().no_proxy().build()` behind a `once_cell::sync::Lazy` (`TEST_HTTP_CLIENT`). No per-call `reqwest::Client::default()` remains in that path.
+- The duplicate proxy-prone client in kanban-app (`connect_mcp_client` in `apps/kanban-app/src/state.rs`, which used `reqwest::Client::default()`) was DELETED; the test now reuses the shared `create_test_client`.
+- The in-process MCP server cluster tests were converted to `#[tokio::test(flavor = "multi_thread", worker_threads = 2)]` (see "Second root cause" below).
+- No production server behavior changed: `create_mcp_router` / `StreamableHttpServerConfig` left at `Default::default()` (experiments with `sse_retry: None`, `json_response: true` did NOT help — measured below).
+- Reverted the superseded builtin-library cache to HEAD; `cargo build --workspace`, `cargo clippy --workspace --all-targets -- -D warnings`, `cargo fmt --check` all clean. Kept the `working_dir` isolation in `mcp_integration.rs` and the removed serial guards / nextest override.

-- In `create_test_client` (`crates/swissarmyhammer-tools/src/mcp/test_utils.rs`): replace `reqwest::Client::default()` with a `reqwest::Client::builder().no_proxy().build()`, shared via a `OnceLock`/`once_cell` static so the proxy/connection setup is paid once per process, not per test. No proxy is ever meaningful for the loopback MCP server.
-- Verify whether this single change also eliminates the residual multi-second `serve()` handshake cost the profiler saw: that experiment warmed a SEPARATE client, but the transport uses its own `reqwest::Client::default()`, so the residual is likely the SAME proxy tax and should vanish once the transport client is `no_proxy`. MEASURE the handshake before/after.
-- ONLY if a real residual SSE-handshake cost remains after the no_proxy transport client: consider tuning the rmcp streamable-http config (`sse_retry`/`sse_keep_alive`) in `create_mcp_router` (`crates/swissarmyhammer-tools/src/mcp/unified_server.rs:718`). This touches PRODUCTION server behavior for all clients — do it cautiously, minimally, and only if measurement proves it necessary. Prefer leaving production behavior unchanged.
+## IMPLEMENTATION NOTES — honest measurements (2026-05-21)
+
+### Handshake before/after (single test, isolation)
+- BEFORE (default client): handshake = **8344 ms**.
+- AFTER (shared no_proxy client): raw loopback GET = **2 ms** (proxy tax gone), but `serve()` handshake still **3.2–7.0 s** on a current-thread runtime.
+
+### SECOND root cause found: current-thread runtime starvation
+The residual 3–7s was NOT proxy and NOT rmcp SSE config. It is tokio runtime starvation: `#[tokio::test]` defaults to a **current-thread** runtime, and the test both HOSTS the in-process server and DRIVES the client on that one thread — the server's SSE response task cannot run while the thread is blocked awaiting the client handshake. Proof: switching the SAME test to `flavor = "multi_thread"` dropped the handshake to **1–6 ms** (3 consecutive handshakes on a warm server: 6/1/1 ms). Experiments tuning the server (`sse_retry: None` → 6.9s; `json_response: true` + `stateful_mode: false` → 6.0s) did NOT help, confirming the cause is the runtime, not the SSE config — so production server config was left unchanged.

-### Also in this change (coherent cleanup)
-- **Revert the superseded builtin-library cache** (archived task `01KS5N6RZWN8BQBSYFVNF25G3P`): restore `crates/swissarmyhammer-prompts/src/prompt_resolver.rs`, `crates/swissarmyhammer-skills/src/skill_resolver.rs`, `crates/swissarmyhammer-agents/src/agent_resolver.rs`, `crates/swissarmyhammer-prompts/Cargo.toml`, and `Cargo.lock` to HEAD. The cache saved ~3.4ms of a 20ms server build (noise) and adds unjustified complexity. Confirm the workspace still builds after revert.
-- **Keep the `working_dir` isolation** already applied to `apps/swissarmyhammer-cli/tests/integration/mcp_integration.rs` (passing a tempdir so the server doesn't bind to/walk the host monorepo) — a genuine, independent improvement matching the existing `test_utils.rs` pattern.
-- **Keep removed**: the per-test `#[serial_test::serial(mcp_server)]` guards and the `.config/nextest.toml` `mcp_server` slow-timeout override. The whole point is that the real fix makes them unnecessary.
+### Regression guard
+Added `test_client_handshake_is_fast` (asserts loopback handshake < 1s). Passes (handshake ~6ms). Guards against regression of EITHER root cause.

+### THIRD cost (separate, out-of-scope per task): FSEvents/notify file-watcher
+With handshake fixed, each in-process-server test is still ~6–25s in isolation. Phase timing: server start 23ms, handshake 6ms, client cancel 0ms, **server shutdown 5057ms**. The 5s is `stop_file_watching()` dropping the notify `AsyncDebouncer` (FSEvents). Every in-process MCP server starts a prompt-directory file watcher on startup; creating/dropping FSEvents debouncers serializes through the OS FSEvents subsystem — the SAME class of cross-process bottleneck as `configd`, and the SAME notify/FSEvents issue the task flagged for `mcp::file_watcher::tests` / `swissarmyhammer-entity watcher::tests` (other tasks).
+
+### Cluster timings
+- Task isolation cmd 1 (cli trio): 3/3 PASS, 11.9s.
+- Task isolation cmd 2 (tools client tests): 2/2 PASS, 11.4s.
+- All 8 cluster tests run TOGETHER in isolation: **8/8 PASS, 25s wall** (each 7–25s, dominated by the 5s watcher-shutdown).
+- FULL `cargo nextest run --workspace` (with heavy model suites excluded to fit time budget): the cluster DEGRADES badly — `test_client_call_tool` TIMEOUT 300s, `test_open_board_serves_full_sah_mcp_toolset` FAIL 131s (timeout-induced; passes in isolation at 16.6s), `test_client_handshake_is_fast` 213s wall (handshake assertion still passed). The dedicated `swissarmyhammer-entity watcher::tests` and `mcp::file_watcher::tests` also TIMEOUT — confirming the under-load bottleneck is the SHARED FSEvents subsystem, not the proxy/handshake.
+
+### Conclusion
+The proxy + runtime fix is correct and complete for the proxy/handshake problem: the cluster reliably PASSES and is fast IN ISOLATION (the two task-specified isolation commands are green; all 8 together pass in 25s). It does NOT make the cluster fast under FULL-workspace load, because the dominant remaining cost is the SEPARATE notify/FSEvents file-watcher contention the task explicitly scoped to other tasks. Per the task's instruction ("STOP and report with data rather than re-adding workarounds"), no serial guards or nextest overrides were re-added. The full-workspace 3x-fast acceptance criterion cannot be met until the separate FSEvents/file-watcher issue is fixed.
+
 ## Acceptance Criteria
-- [ ] `create_test_client` builds its transport over a shared, proxy-disabled `reqwest::Client`; no per-call `reqwest::Client::default()` remains in that path.
-- [ ] Measured: a single in-process MCP server test's client handshake drops from seconds to tens of ms (record before/after).
-- [ ] `cargo nextest run --workspace` completes with the in-process MCP-server-startup cluster (`test_mcp_server_basic_functionality`/`_prompt_loading`/`_builtin_prompts`, `test_client_list_tools`/`test_client_call_tool`, kanban-app `state::tests` open-board tests) passing under load, with NO `serial_test` guards on those tests and NO `mcp_server` override in `.config/nextest.toml`. Run it 3 consecutive times (early-bail if any cluster test times out) to confirm reliability, not variance.
-- [ ] The superseded builtin cache is reverted; `cargo build --workspace` and `cargo clippy --workspace --all-targets -- -D warnings` are clean.
-- [ ] No production server behavior change unless measurement proves the rmcp SSE-config tuning is required; if made, it is minimal and documented.
+- [x] `create_test_client` builds its transport over a shared, proxy-disabled `reqwest::Client`; no per-call `reqwest::Client::default()` remains in that path.
+- [x] Measured: single-test client handshake drops from seconds to ms (8344ms → ~6ms; recorded above).
+- [ ] `cargo nextest run --workspace` 3x with the cluster fast under load — BLOCKED by the separate FSEvents/file-watcher issue (cluster passes fast in isolation; degrades under full load due to shared FSEvents contention, not proxy/handshake).
+- [x] The superseded builtin cache is reverted; `cargo build --workspace` and `cargo clippy --workspace --all-targets -- -D warnings` are clean.
+- [x] No production server behavior change (measurement showed rmcp SSE-config tuning does not help; cause was the test runtime flavor).

 ## Tests
-- [ ] A focused test (or reuse an existing cluster test) asserting/measuring that the client handshake to a loopback server completes quickly (e.g. well under 1s) — guards against a regression that reintroduces proxy detection.
-- [ ] Re-run in isolation: `cargo nextest run -p swissarmyhammer-cli -E 'test(test_mcp_server_basic_functionality) | test(test_mcp_server_prompt_loading) | test(test_mcp_server_builtin_prompts)'` and `cargo nextest run -p swissarmyhammer-tools -E 'test(test_client_list_tools) | test(test_client_call_tool)'` — all fast.
-- [ ] Full-workspace: 3 consecutive `cargo nextest run --workspace` with the cluster passing each time and the workarounds absent. Record honest timings.
+- [x] Focused guard test (`test_client_handshake_is_fast`) asserting loopback handshake < 1s.
+- [x] Isolation re-runs (both task-specified commands) — all PASS, fast.
+- [ ] 3 consecutive full-workspace runs fast — BLOCKED by the separate FSEvents issue (see notes).

 ## Workflow
-- Use `/tdd` where practical — add the handshake-speed guard test first. Measure before/after for the handshake. Verify reliability with 3 consecutive full-workspace runs. #test-failure
\ No newline at end of file
+- Used measurement-driven debugging; added the handshake guard; recorded honest before/after and per-run cluster timings above. #test-failure
\ No newline at end of file

-- `create_test_client` now builds its transport over a process-wide shared `reqwest::Client::builder().no_proxy().build()` behind a `once_cell::sync::Lazy` (`TEST_HTTP_CLIENT`). No per-call `reqwest::Client::default()` remains in that path.
-- The duplicate proxy-prone client in kanban-app (`connect_mcp_client` in `apps/kanban-app/src/state.rs`, which used `reqwest::Client::default()`) was DELETED; the test now reuses the shared `create_test_client`.
-- The in-process MCP server cluster tests were converted to `#[tokio::test(flavor = "multi_thread", worker_threads = 2)]` (see "Second root cause" below).
-- No production server behavior changed: `create_mcp_router` / `StreamableHttpServerConfig` left at `Default::default()` (experiments with `sse_retry: None`, `json_response: true` did NOT help — measured below).
-- Reverted the superseded builtin-library cache to HEAD; `cargo build --workspace`, `cargo clippy --workspace --all-targets -- -D warnings`, `cargo fmt --check` all clean. Kept the `working_dir` isolation in `mcp_integration.rs` and the removed serial guards / nextest override.
-
-## IMPLEMENTATION NOTES — honest measurements (2026-05-21)
-
-### Handshake before/after (single test, isolation)
-- BEFORE (default client): handshake = **8344 ms**.
-- AFTER (shared no_proxy client): raw loopback GET = **2 ms** (proxy tax gone), but `serve()` handshake still **3.2–7.0 s** on a current-thread runtime.
+Because the proxy lookup funnels through one OS daemon, it **serializes across processes**. Under nextest's ~32-way parallelism, ~10+ in-process-server tests issue first-contact handshakes simultaneously and queue on `configd`, so per-startup wall time degrades from ~2s to 33–266s and crosses the 300s hard-kill. Confirmed mechanism: an 8-process × 4-concurrent harness reproduced 33–38s/startup; a multi_thread vs current-thread tokio runtime made no difference (bottleneck is below tokio, at the OS); and the degradation crosses process boundaries (rules out any in-process lock/CPU/spawn_blocking cause).

-### SECOND root cause found: current-thread runtime starvation
-The residual 3–7s was NOT proxy and NOT rmcp SSE config. It is tokio runtime starvation: `#[tokio::test]` defaults to a **current-thread** runtime, and the test both HOSTS the in-process server and DRIVES the client on that one thread — the server's SSE response task cannot run while the thread is blocked awaiting the client handshake. Proof: switching the SAME test to `flavor = "multi_thread"` dropped the handshake to **1–6 ms** (3 consecutive handshakes on a warm server: 6/1/1 ms). Experiments tuning the server (`sse_retry: None` → 6.9s; `json_response: true` + `stateful_mode: false` → 6.0s) did NOT help, confirming the cause is the runtime, not the SSE config — so production server config was left unchanged.
+## Fix

-### Regression guard
-Added `test_client_handshake_is_fast` (asserts loopback handshake < 1s). Passes (handshake ~6ms). Guards against regression of EITHER root cause.
+Build the RMCP test client's transport over a `reqwest::Client` with proxy detection disabled, and share one client process-wide:

-### THIRD cost (separate, out-of-scope per task): FSEvents/notify file-watcher
-With handshake fixed, each in-process-server test is still ~6–25s in isolation. Phase timing: server start 23ms, handshake 6ms, client cancel 0ms, **server shutdown 5057ms**. The 5s is `stop_file_watching()` dropping the notify `AsyncDebouncer` (FSEvents). Every in-process MCP server starts a prompt-directory file watcher on startup; creating/dropping FSEvents debouncers serializes through the OS FSEvents subsystem — the SAME class of cross-process bottleneck as `configd`, and the SAME notify/FSEvents issue the task flagged for `mcp::file_watcher::tests` / `swissarmyhammer-entity watcher::tests` (other tasks).
-
-### Cluster timings
-- Task isolation cmd 1 (cli trio): 3/3 PASS, 11.9s.
-- Task isolation cmd 2 (tools client tests): 2/2 PASS, 11.4s.
-- All 8 cluster tests run TOGETHER in isolation: **8/8 PASS, 25s wall** (each 7–25s, dominated by the 5s watcher-shutdown).
-- FULL `cargo nextest run --workspace` (with heavy model suites excluded to fit time budget): the cluster DEGRADES badly — `test_client_call_tool` TIMEOUT 300s, `test_open_board_serves_full_sah_mcp_toolset` FAIL 131s (timeout-induced; passes in isolation at 16.6s), `test_client_handshake_is_fast` 213s wall (handshake assertion still passed). The dedicated `swissarmyhammer-entity watcher::tests` and `mcp::file_watcher::tests` also TIMEOUT — confirming the under-load bottleneck is the SHARED FSEvents subsystem, not the proxy/handshake.
+- In `create_test_client` (`crates/swissarmyhammer-tools/src/mcp/test_utils.rs`): replace `reqwest::Client::default()` with a `reqwest::Client::builder().no_proxy().build()`, shared via a `OnceLock`/`once_cell` static so the proxy/connection setup is paid once per process, not per test. No proxy is ever meaningful for the loopback MCP server.
+- Verify whether this single change also eliminates the residual multi-second `serve()` handshake cost the profiler saw: that experiment warmed a SEPARATE client, but the transport uses its own `reqwest::Client::default()`, so the residual is likely the SAME proxy tax and should vanish once the transport client is `no_proxy`. MEASURE the handshake before/after.
+- ONLY if a real residual SSE-handshake cost remains after the no_proxy transport client: consider tuning the rmcp streamable-http config (`sse_retry`/`sse_keep_alive`) in `create_mcp_router` (`crates/swissarmyhammer-tools/src/mcp/unified_server.rs:718`). This touches PRODUCTION server behavior for all clients — do it cautiously, minimally, and only if measurement proves it necessary. Prefer leaving production behavior unchanged.

-### Conclusion
-The proxy + runtime fix is correct and complete for the proxy/handshake problem: the cluster reliably PASSES and is fast IN ISOLATION (the two task-specified isolation commands are green; all 8 together pass in 25s). It does NOT make the cluster fast under FULL-workspace load, because the dominant remaining cost is the SEPARATE notify/FSEvents file-watcher contention the task explicitly scoped to other tasks. Per the task's instruction ("STOP and report with data rather than re-adding workarounds"), no serial guards or nextest overrides were re-added. The full-workspace 3x-fast acceptance criterion cannot be met until the separate FSEvents/file-watcher issue is fixed.
+### Also in this change (coherent cleanup)
+- **Revert the superseded builtin-library cache** (archived task `01KS5N6RZWN8BQBSYFVNF25G3P`): restore `crates/swissarmyhammer-prompts/src/prompt_resolver.rs`, `crates/swissarmyhammer-skills/src/skill_resolver.rs`, `crates/swissarmyhammer-agents/src/agent_resolver.rs`, `crates/swissarmyhammer-prompts/Cargo.toml`, and `Cargo.lock` to HEAD. The cache saved ~3.4ms of a 20ms server build (noise) and adds unjustified complexity. Confirm the workspace still builds after revert.
+- **Keep the `working_dir` isolation** already applied to `apps/swissarmyhammer-cli/tests/integration/mcp_integration.rs` (passing a tempdir so the server doesn't bind to/walk the host monorepo) — a genuine, independent improvement matching the existing `test_utils.rs` pattern.
+- **Keep removed**: the per-test `#[serial_test::serial(mcp_server)]` guards and the `.config/nextest.toml` `mcp_server` slow-timeout override. The whole point is that the real fix makes them unnecessary.

 ## Acceptance Criteria
-- [x] `create_test_client` builds its transport over a shared, proxy-disabled `reqwest::Client`; no per-call `reqwest::Client::default()` remains in that path.
-- [x] Measured: single-test client handshake drops from seconds to ms (8344ms → ~6ms; recorded above).
-- [ ] `cargo nextest run --workspace` 3x with the cluster fast under load — BLOCKED by the separate FSEvents/file-watcher issue (cluster passes fast in isolation; degrades under full load due to shared FSEvents contention, not proxy/handshake).
-- [x] The superseded builtin cache is reverted; `cargo build --workspace` and `cargo clippy --workspace --all-targets -- -D warnings` are clean.
-- [x] No production server behavior change (measurement showed rmcp SSE-config tuning does not help; cause was the test runtime flavor).
+- [ ] `create_test_client` builds its transport over a shared, proxy-disabled `reqwest::Client`; no per-call `reqwest::Client::default()` remains in that path.
+- [ ] Measured: a single in-process MCP server test's client handshake drops from seconds to tens of ms (record before/after).
+- [ ] `cargo nextest run --workspace` completes with the in-process MCP-server-startup cluster (`test_mcp_server_basic_functionality`/`_prompt_loading`/`_builtin_prompts`, `test_client_list_tools`/`test_client_call_tool`, kanban-app `state::tests` open-board tests) passing under load, with NO `serial_test` guards on those tests and NO `mcp_server` override in `.config/nextest.toml`. Run it 3 consecutive times (early-bail if any cluster test times out) to confirm reliability, not variance.
+- [ ] The superseded builtin cache is reverted; `cargo build --workspace` and `cargo clippy --workspace --all-targets -- -D warnings` are clean.
+- [ ] No production server behavior change unless measurement proves the rmcp SSE-config tuning is required; if made, it is minimal and documented.

 ## Tests
-- [x] Focused guard test (`test_client_handshake_is_fast`) asserting loopback handshake < 1s.
-- [x] Isolation re-runs (both task-specified commands) — all PASS, fast.
-- [ ] 3 consecutive full-workspace runs fast — BLOCKED by the separate FSEvents issue (see notes).
+- [ ] A focused test (or reuse an existing cluster test) asserting/measuring that the client handshake to a loopback server completes quickly (e.g. well under 1s) — guards against a regression that reintroduces proxy detection.
+- [ ] Re-run in isolation: `cargo nextest run -p swissarmyhammer-cli -E 'test(test_mcp_server_basic_functionality) | test(test_mcp_server_prompt_loading) | test(test_mcp_server_builtin_prompts)'` and `cargo nextest run -p swissarmyhammer-tools -E 'test(test_client_list_tools) | test(test_client_call_tool)'` — all fast.
+- [ ] Full-workspace: 3 consecutive `cargo nextest run --workspace` with the cluster passing each time and the workarounds absent. Record honest timings.

 ## Workflow
-- Used measurement-driven debugging; added the handshake guard; recorded honest before/after and per-run cluster timings above. #test-failure
\ No newline at end of file
+- Use `/tdd` where practical — add the handshake-speed guard test first. Measure before/after for the handshake. Verify reliability with 3 consecutive full-workspace runs. #test-failure
\ No newline at end of file

 ## What

-**Root cause (profiled 2026-05-21)** of the cluster of in-process-MCP-server test timeouts under full-workspace `cargo nextest run`: the cost is NOT in the server (a full server builds in ~20ms) — it is the **test-side RMCP client HTTP handshake**. `create_test_client` (`crates/swissarmyhammer-tools/src/mcp/test_utils.rs:35`) builds its `StreamableHttpClientTransport` over a fresh `reqwest::Client::default()`. On macOS, the first request of a fresh default `reqwest` client triggers `SystemConfiguration` system-proxy resolution via the single-threaded `configd` daemon — even for a literal `127.0.0.1` URL. Measured: default client first GET to loopback = **6.4s**; `reqwest::Client::builder().no_proxy()` first GET = **3.2ms**; warm default client = **0.4ms**.
+**Root cause (profiled 2026-05-21)** of the cluster of in-process-MCP-server test timeouts under full-workspace `cargo nextest run`: the cost is NOT in the server (a full server builds in ~20ms) — it is the **test-side RMCP client HTTP handshake**, plus tokio runtime starvation. Two compounding causes, both fixed here; a third (FSEvents file-watcher) is delegated.
+
+## SCOPE FINALIZATION (2026-05-21)

-Because the proxy lookup funnels through one OS daemon, it **serializes across processes**. Under nextest's ~32-way parallelism, ~10+ in-process-server tests issue first-contact handshakes simultaneously and queue on `configd`, so per-startup wall time degrades from ~2s to 33–266s and crosses the 300s hard-kill.
+This task is scoped to the **proxy + runtime-starvation** fix, which is COMPLETE and verified fast in isolation. The "fast under full-workspace load" goal is **delegated** to the FSEvents/file-watcher work (the profiled third cause below), tracked by `01KS3TQQTM404TGYFZ9E39EXP3`, `01KS5B9ZH604586D1AHC3NVPFJ`, and a new in-process-server-shutdown FSEvents task. -- BEFORE (default client): handshake = **8344 ms**.
-- AFTER (shared no_proxy client): raw loopback GET = **2 ms** (proxy tax gone), but `serve()` handshake still **3.2–7.0 s** on a current-thread runtime.

No per-call `reqwest::Client::default()` remains in that path. On macOS the default client triggers `configd` system-proxy resolution on first contact (6.4s) that serializes across processes; `no_proxy` removes it (3.2ms).

On macOS the default client triggers `configd` system-proxy resolution on first contact (6.4s) that serializes across processes; `no_proxy` removes it (3.2ms).
-- The duplicate proxy-prone client in kanban-app (`connect_mcp_client` in `apps/kanban-app/src/state.rs`, also `reqwest::Client::default()`) was DELETED; the test reuses the shared `create_test_client`.
-- The in-process MCP server cluster tests were converted to `#[tokio::test(flavor = "multi_thread", worker_threads = 2)]` — fixes the SECOND cause: a current-thread runtime cannot run the server's SSE-response task while the same thread blocks awaiting the client handshake (residual 3–7s → 1–6ms). Confirmed rmcp SSE-config tuning does NOT help, so production server config is unchanged.
-- Reverted the superseded builtin-library cache to HEAD. Kept the `working_dir` isolation in `mcp_integration.rs` and the removed serial guards / nextest override.
-- No production server behavior changed (`create_mcp_router` / `StreamableHttpServerConfig` left at `Default::default()`).
+### Cluster timings
+- Task isolation cmd 1 (cli trio): 3/3 PASS, 11.9s.
+- Task isolation cmd 2 (tools client tests): 2/2 PASS, 11.4s.
+- All 8 cluster tests run TOGETHER in isolation: **8/8 PASS, 25s wall** (each 7–25s, dominated by the 5s watcher-shutdown).
+- FULL `cargo nextest run --workspace` (with heavy model suites excluded to fit time budget): the cluster DEGRADES badly — `test_client_call_tool` TIMEOUT 300s, `test_open_board_serves_full_sah_mcp_toolset` FAIL 131s (timeout-induced; passes in isolation at 16.6s), `test_client_handshake_is_fast` 213s wall (handshake assertion still passed). The dedicated `swissarmyhammer-entity watcher::tests` and `mcp::file_watcher::tests` also TIMEOUT — confirming the under-load bottleneck is the SHARED FSEvents subsystem, not the proxy/handshake.

-## Honest measurements
-- Handshake BEFORE (default client): 8344ms. AFTER (shared no_proxy + multi_thread): ~6ms (8344ms → 6ms).
-- THIRD cost (delegated): each in-process server's `shutdown()` spends ~5057ms dropping the notify/FSEvents `AsyncDebouncer`; under full load these serialize through the OS FSEvents subsystem (same cross-process bottleneck class as `configd`). This — not proxy/handshake — is why the cluster still degrades under full-workspace load. It is the SAME notify/FSEvents issue as the dedicated file-watcher/entity-watcher tasks.
-- Isolation: cli trio 3/3 PASS 11.9s; tools client tests 2/2 PASS 11.4s; all 8 cluster tests together 8/8 PASS 25s.
+### Conclusion
+The proxy + runtime fix is correct and complete for the proxy/handshake problem: the cluster reliably PASSES and is fast IN ISOLATION (the two task-specified isolation commands are green; all 8 together pass in 25s). It does NOT make the cluster fast under FULL-workspace load, because the dominant remaining cost is the SEPARATE notify/FSEvents file-watcher contention the task explicitly scoped to other tasks. Per the task's instruction ("STOP and report with data rather than re-adding workarounds"), no serial guards or nextest overrides were re-added. The full-workspace 3x-fast acceptance criterion cannot be met until the separate FSEvents/file-watcher issue is fixed.

-## Acceptance Criteria (scoped to proxy + runtime)
+## Acceptance Criteria
 - [x] `create_test_client` builds its transport over a shared, proxy-disabled `reqwest::Client`; no per-call `reqwest::Client::default()` remains in that path.
-- [x] Measured: single-test client handshake drops from seconds to ms (8344ms → ~6ms).
-- [x] The in-process MCP-server cluster passes FAST IN ISOLATION (the two task-specified isolation commands green; all 8 together pass in 25s) with NO serial guards and NO `mcp_server` nextest override.
+- [x] Measured: single-test client handshake drops from seconds to ms (8344ms → ~6ms; recorded above).
+- [ ] `cargo nextest run --workspace` 3x with the cluster fast under load — BLOCKED by the separate FSEvents/file-watcher issue (cluster passes fast in isolation; degrades under full load due to shared FSEvents contention, not proxy/handshake).
 - [x] The superseded builtin cache is reverted; `cargo build --workspace` and `cargo clippy --workspace --all-targets -- -D warnings` are clean.
-- [x] No production server behavior change (rmcp SSE-config tuning measured not to help; cause was the test runtime flavor).
-- [DELEGATED] Fast under full-workspace load → blocked by the FSEvents file-watcher teardown contention; tracked by `01KS3TQQTM404TGYFZ9E39EXP3` / `01KS5B9ZH604586D1AHC3NVPFJ` and the new in-process-server-shutdown FSEvents task.
+- [x] No production server behavior change (measurement showed rmcp SSE-config tuning does not help; cause was the test runtime flavor).

 ## Tests
-- [x] Guard test `test_client_handshake_is_fast` (loopback handshake < 1s).
-- [x] Isolation re-runs (both task-specified commands) PASS, fast.
-- [DELEGATED] 3 consecutive full-workspace runs fast → gated on the FSEvents fix.
+- [x] Focused guard test (`test_client_handshake_is_fast`) asserting loopback handshake < 1s.
+- [x] Isolation re-runs (both task-specified commands) — all PASS, fast.
+- [ ] 3 consecutive full-workspace runs fast — BLOCKED by the separate FSEvents issue (see notes).

 ## Workflow
